items.stem: The hawker was very busy. 'Busy' means almost the same as ___.
items.rationale: 'Busy' and 'occupied' both mean having a lot to do.

items.stem: The boy felt happy on his birthday. 'Happy' means almost the same as ___.
The MRT was crowded in the morning. 'Crowded' means almost the same as ___.
items.rationale: 'Crowded' and 'packed' both mean full of people.

items.stem: She spoke softly so as not to wake the baby. 'Softly' means almost the same as ___.
items.rationale: 'Softly' and 'quietly' both mean without much noise.

items.stem: The test was difficult. 'Difficult' means almost the same as ___.
The firefighter was brave. 'Brave' means almost the same as ___.
The old man walked slowly along the corridor. 'Slowly' means almost the same as ___.
items.rationale: 'Slowly' and 'unhurriedly' both mean at a low speed.

items.stem: Her room was very tidy. 'Tidy' means almost the same as ___.
The laksa was tasty. 'Tasty' means almost the same as ___.
items.rationale: 'Tasty' and 'delicious' both mean full of flavour.

items.stem: The teacher was pleased with the class. 'Pleased' means almost the same as ___.
items.rationale: 'Pleased' and 'satisfied' both mean happy with something.

VOC-synonyms.iCanStatement: I can choose a word that means almost the same.
VOC-synonyms.explanation: A synonym is a word with a similar meaning. Read the whole sentence, then pick the choice that could replace the word without changing what it means.

workedExamples.note: 'big' and 'large' mean about the same size.
